name: Render unto Caesar
notes: The threefold synoptic account of the question of tribute, where adversaries try to trap Jesus between Rome and the people. All three share the same core: a plot to entangle him in his words, a flattering preface, the lawful-tribute question, Jesus perceiving their hypocrisy, the penny with its image and superscription, the answer to render unto Caesar and unto God, and their marvelling withdrawal. Matthew names the Pharisees' disciples with the Herodians and notes they marvelled and went their way; Mark presses the sharper double form 'Shall we give or shall we not give'; Luke supplies the vantage of spies feigning to be just men, aiming to deliver him to the governor's authority, and that they held their peace. These are complementary vantages of one encounter, each preserving detail the others leave out.
